qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Stored XSS in HTML cell rendering enables same-origin data theft
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a frontend HTML-rendering bug that stores attacker content through /dtale/update-formats and executes it via GridCell dangerouslySetInnerHTML, while the advisory fixes code injection in /save-column-filter by validating filter/query input before DataFrame.query() is reached.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's /save-column-filter code-injection root cause. The only final qualifying finding is stored XSS in HTML cell rendering, which affects different routes and code paths.
evidence: The advisory summary and fix commit tie the vulnerability to /save-column-filter/<data_id>, unsafe column-filter query construction in dtale/column_filters.py, and execution in dtale/query.py via pandas DataFrame.query().
The final qualifying finding cites /dtale/upload, /dtale/edit-cell/<data_id>, /dtale/update-formats/<data_id>, and frontend GridCell dangerouslySetInnerHTML as its exploit path and code locations.
The advisory patch adds validation for dangerous filter values and query strings before DataFrame.query() is called; it does not touch the HTML rendering path used by the finding.
